Quality Control

EUT046 - Version 2

Imke Bruns (0000-0001-9681-6693)

2025-05-23

1 Set up analysis

1.1 Load packages

1.1.1 CRAN

Install and load CRAN packages.

# if(!require(pacman, quietly = TRUE)){ install.packages('pacman') }

pacman::p_load(tidyverse, data.table, ggfortify, here)

1.1.2 BioManager

Install and load Bioconductor packages.

# if (!require('BiocManager', quietly = TRUE)){ install.packages('BiocManager')
# } if (!require(DESeq2, quietly = TRUE)){ BiocManager::install('DESeq2') }

library(DESeq2)

1.2 Load functions

Load manual functions we need in the script.

source(file.path(here(), "functions.R"), local = knitr::knit_global())

1.3 Set color scheme

Set standard color scheme for plots.

# color list for plotting
color25 <- c(
  "dodgerblue2", "#E31A1C", # red
  "green4",
  "#6A3D9A", # purple
  "#FF7F00", # orange
  "black", "gold1",
  "skyblue2", "#FB9A99", # lt pink
  "palegreen2",
  "#CAB2D6", # lt purple
  "#FDBF6F", # lt orange
  "gray70", "khaki2",
  "maroon", "orchid1", "deeppink1", "blue1", "steelblue4",
  "darkturquoise", "green1", "yellow4", "yellow3",
  "darkorange4", "brown", "forestgreen"
)

1.4 Set directories

Set directories.

# data directory
if (!dir.exists(paste0("data/"))) {
    dir.create(path = file.path(here(), "data"))
}
## Warning in dir.create(path = file.path(here(), "data")):
## 'J:\Workgroups\FWN\LACDR\TOX\VDWATER\Imke_Bruns\05_GitHub\PARC-SystemsTox\CS-ab_initio\LU_DEG_analysis\data'
## already exists
dataDir <- file.path(here(), "data", studyNR)

# input directory
if (!dir.exists(paste0("input/", studyNR, "/"))) {
    dir.create(path = file.path(here(), "input", studyNR))
}
## Warning in dir.create(path = file.path(here(), "input", studyNR)):
## 'J:\Workgroups\FWN\LACDR\TOX\VDWATER\Imke_Bruns\05_GitHub\PARC-SystemsTox\CS-ab_initio\LU_DEG_analysis\input\EUT046'
## already exists
inputDir <- file.path(here(), "input", studyNR)

# output directory
if (!dir.exists(paste0("output/", studyNR, "/"))) {
    dir.create(path = file.path(here(), "output", studyNR))
}
## Warning in dir.create(path = file.path(here(), "output", studyNR)):
## 'J:\Workgroups\FWN\LACDR\TOX\VDWATER\Imke_Bruns\05_GitHub\PARC-SystemsTox\CS-ab_initio\LU_DEG_analysis\output\EUT046'
## already exists
outputDir <- file.path(here(), "output", studyNR)

# plot directory
if (!dir.exists(paste0("figures/", studyNR, "/"))) {
    dir.create(path = file.path(here(), "figures", studyNR))
}
## Warning in dir.create(path = file.path(here(), "figures", studyNR)):
## 'J:\Workgroups\FWN\LACDR\TOX\VDWATER\Imke_Bruns\05_GitHub\PARC-SystemsTox\CS-ab_initio\LU_DEG_analysis\figures\EUT046'
## already exists
plotDir <- file.path(here(), "figures", studyNR)

1.5 Thresholds for library size

Here, we set the threshold for the library size filter. We set this to be 20% of the expected read depth which is in this case 3 000 000.

expectedReadDepth = 3e+06
libSizeFilter = 0.2 * expectedReadDepth

1.6 Import data

Import needed data files.

# raw counts
rawcounts = read.csv(file.path(inputDir, paste0(studyNR, "_counts_per_gene_per_sample.csv"))) %>%
    dplyr::rename(probe_id = Column1)


# metadata
metadata = read.csv(file.path(inputDir, paste0(studyNR, "_metadata.csv"))) %>%
    mutate(TREATMENT_ID = gsub(" ", "", TREATMENT_ID), MEAN_ID = if_else(COMPOUND %in%
        c("DMEM", "DMSO", "NaCl"), true = TREATMENT_ID, false = paste0(TREATMENT_ID,
        "_R", REPLICATE))) %>%
    mutate(CONCENTRATION = factor(CONCENTRATION, levels = c("0uM", "0.1uM", "0.5uM",
        "1uM", "2.5uM", "5uM", "10uM", "20uM", "30uM", "50uM")), TIMEPOINT = factor(TIMEPOINT,
        levels = c("0hr", "4hr", "8hr", "16hr", "24hr", "48hr", "72hr")), DOSE = as.numeric(gsub("uM",
        "", CONCENTRATION)))

# manifest
manifest = read.delim(file.path(dirname(dataDir), "Manifests", "Temposeq_manifest_Human_WT_1.2_realignment_96percent_2023-05-15.txt"))

2 Quality control

2.1 Probe QC

We start with the quality control for the probes.

2.1.1 STEP 1: Probe alignment

Here, we align the probes with the manifest.

rawcounts_entrezid = rawcounts %>%
    left_join(manifest %>%
        select(Probe_ID, gene_symbol_entrez_id), by = c(probe_id = "Probe_ID")) %>%
    select(probe_id, gene_symbol_entrez_id, everything())

2.1.2 STEP 2: Relevance filter

Apply relevance filter adapted from the R-ODAF pipeline (see: https://doi.org/10.1016/j.yrtph.2022.105143)

relevance_summary = relevance_filter(countdata = apply(rawcounts_entrezid %>%
    column_to_rownames("probe_id") %>%
    select(-gene_symbol_entrez_id), 2, cpm_normalization) %>%
    as.data.frame(), metadata = metadata)
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'probe_id'. You can override using the
## `.groups` argument.
rawcounts_entrezid_relevanceFilter = rawcounts_entrezid %>%
    filter(probe_id %in% (relevance_summary %>%
        filter(keep == TRUE) %>%
        pull(probe_id)))
# %>% select(-probe_id)


cat("The relevance filter removed", nrow(rawcounts_entrezid) - nrow(rawcounts_entrezid_relevanceFilter),
    "probes.", nrow(rawcounts_entrezid_relevanceFilter), "are left for further analysis.")
## The relevance filter removed 8477 probes. 12634 are left for further analysis.

2.1.3 STEP 3: Spurious spikes

Group replicate groups + probe_id, calculate the ratio between the maximum of probeX and the sum of probeX for each replicate group. If this ratio exceeds 1.4*number of replicates^-0.66, flag. The flagged genes are in the spike_summary table.

log2cpmcounts_entrezid_relevanceFilter_long = log2(apply(rawcounts_entrezid_relevanceFilter %>%
    column_to_rownames("probe_id") %>%
    select(-gene_symbol_entrez_id), 2, cpm_normalization) + 1) %>%
    as.data.frame() %>%
    rownames_to_column("probe_id") %>%
    mutate(gene_symbol_entrez_id = rawcounts_entrezid_relevanceFilter$gene_symbol_entrez_id) %>%
    pivot_longer(-c(gene_symbol_entrez_id, probe_id), names_to = "SAMPLE_ID", values_to = "log2cpm") %>%
    left_join(metadata %>%
        select(SAMPLE_ID, TREATMENT_ID), by = "SAMPLE_ID")



# calculate spike ratios and thresholds per gene group
spike_check = log2cpmcounts_entrezid_relevanceFilter_long %>%
    group_by(probe_id, TREATMENT_ID) %>%
    summarize(max_count = max(log2cpm), sum_count = sum(log2cpm), n_samples = n(),
        ratio = if_else(sum_count == 0, true = 0, false = max_count/sum_count), threshold = 1.4 *
            (n_samples^-0.66), spike = ratio >= threshold, .groups = "drop")


# count how many groups have spikes per gene
spike_summary = spike_check %>%
    group_by(probe_id) %>%
    summarize(groups_with_spikes = sum(spike), pass_spike_filter = groups_with_spikes <=
        1, .groups = "drop")

head(spike_summary)
table(spike_summary$pass_spike_filter)
## 
## FALSE  TRUE 
##  2947  9687
spike_flag = spike_summary %>%
    filter(pass_spike_filter == FALSE) %>%
    left_join(log2cpmcounts_entrezid_relevanceFilter_long %>%
        select(probe_id, gene_symbol_entrez_id), by = "probe_id") %>%
    pull(gene_symbol_entrez_id) %>%
    unique()

cat("The spurious spike filter flagged", length(spike_flag), "genes.")
## The spurious spike filter flagged 2804 genes.

2.1.4 STEP 4: Sum probes

We sum the probes targeting the same gene.

# count isomeric probes
cat("Before sum counts isomeric probes, there are", length(rawcounts_entrezid_relevanceFilter$gene_symbol_entrez_id %>%
    duplicated() %>%
    which()), "duplicated gene symbols \n")
## Before sum counts isomeric probes, there are 1101 duplicated gene symbols
rawcounts_entrezid_relevanceFilter_sumProbes = aggregate(rawcounts_entrezid_relevanceFilter %>%
    select(where(is.numeric)), by = list(gene_symbol_entrez_id = rawcounts_entrezid_relevanceFilter$gene_symbol_entrez_id),
    FUN = sum)

cat("After all probe QC steps, there are", nrow(rawcounts_entrezid_relevanceFilter_sumProbes),
    "unique genes left")
## After all probe QC steps, there are 11532 unique genes left

2.1.5 Probe QC output

Generate output files after the probe QC.

# cpm counts
cpmcounts_entrezid_relevancefilter_sumProbes = apply(rawcounts_entrezid_relevanceFilter_sumProbes %>%
    column_to_rownames("gene_symbol_entrez_id"), 2, cpm_normalization) %>%
    as.data.frame() %>%
    rownames_to_column("gene_symbol_entrez_id")

# log2cpm counts
log2cpmcounts_entrezid_relevanceFilter_sumProbes = log2(cpmcounts_entrezid_relevancefilter_sumProbes %>%
    column_to_rownames("gene_symbol_entrez_id") + 1) %>%
    rownames_to_column("gene_symbol_entrez_id")

2.1.6 Visualize in PCA

Show low QC genes in PCA scale

pcaData_beforeProbeFilter = log2(apply(rawcounts %>%
    column_to_rownames("probe_id"), 2, cpm_normalization) + 1) %>%
    as.data.frame() %>%
    rownames_to_column("probe_id") %>%
    mutate(removed_probeQC = if_else(probe_id %in% (relevance_summary %>%
        filter(keep == TRUE) %>%
        pull(probe_id)), true = "passed", false = "notPassed"), flagged_probeQC = if_else(probe_id %in%
        (spike_summary %>%
            filter(pass_spike_filter == TRUE) %>%
            pull(probe_id)), true = "passed", false = "spike_flagged"))


pcaOutput_beforeProbeFilter = prcomp(pcaData_beforeProbeFilter %>%
    select(where(is.numeric)))

autoplot(pcaOutput_beforeProbeFilter, data = pcaData_beforeProbeFilter, color = "removed_probeQC",
    size = 1) + labs(title = "PCA gene expression (log2CPM)", subtitle = "Probes") +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("firebrick", "forestgreen"), breaks = c("notPassed",
        "passed"))

Show flagged genes in PCA scale

autoplot(pcaOutput_beforeProbeFilter, data = pcaData_beforeProbeFilter, color = "flagged_probeQC",
    size = 1) + labs(title = "PCA gene expression (log2CPM)", subtitle = "Probes") +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("orange", "black"), breaks = c("spike_flagged",
        "passed"))

2.1.7 Save output

save(
  # metadata
  metadata,
  # raw counts
  rawcounts_entrezid_relevanceFilter_sumProbes,
  # cpm counts
  cpmcounts_entrezid_relevancefilter_sumProbes,
  # log2cpm counts
  log2cpmcounts_entrezid_relevanceFilter_sumProbes,
  
  file = file.path(outputDir, "updatedVersion", paste0(studyNR, "_dataAfterProbeQC.RData"))
)

2.2 Sample QC

Now we perform the quality control for the samples.

2.2.1 STEP 5: Library size filter (NMR)

Remove samples if library size is < 20% of the intended read depth. The filter has been set on the top of the script.

2.2.1.1 Add library size to metadata

metadata %>%
    mutate(LIBRARY_SIZE = colSums(rawcounts_entrezid_relevanceFilter_sumProbes %>%
        select(where(is.numeric))), LIBRARY_SIZE_PURE = colSums(rawcounts %>%
        select(where(is.numeric)))) -> metadata

2.2.1.2 Plot library size

Also compare pure library size obtained form raw counts with library size after probe QC

# compare pure library size (from raw counts) with library size after probe QC
p1 = ggplot(metadata, aes(x = LIBRARY_SIZE_PURE, y = LIBRARY_SIZE)) + geom_point() +
    geom_abline()

p1

# show differences in table
metadata %>%
    select(LIBRARY_SIZE, LIBRARY_SIZE_PURE) %>%
    head(10)
# plot library size per concentration/timepoint
p2 = ggplot(metadata, aes(x = CONCENTRATION, y = LIBRARY_SIZE, color = LIBRARY_SIZE >
    libSizeFilter, size = LIBRARY_SIZE > libSizeFilter)) + geom_point() + facet_wrap(~TIMEPOINT) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = libSizeFilter, lty = 2, color = "firebrick", linewidth = 0.8) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("firebrick", "black"), breaks = c(FALSE, TRUE)) +
    scale_size_manual(values = c(1, 2.5), breaks = c(TRUE, FALSE)) + theme(legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90)) + labs(title = "Library size per concentration/timepoint")

p2

2.2.1.3 Remove samples

From metadata

cat("We filter all samples with a library size of <", libSizeFilter, "\n")
## We filter all samples with a library size of < 6e+05
metadata_libSize = metadata %>%
    filter(LIBRARY_SIZE >= libSizeFilter)

cat("The following samples have been removed due to low library size:\n", paste(metadata %>%
    filter(LIBRARY_SIZE < libSizeFilter) %>%
    pull(SAMPLE_ID), collapse = ", "))
## The following samples have been removed due to low library size:
##  S_8h_Cis8_Rep1

From counts

# raw counts
rawcounts_entrezid_relevanceFilter_sumProbes_libSize = rawcounts_entrezid_relevanceFilter_sumProbes %>%
    select(gene_symbol_entrez_id, all_of(metadata_libSize$SAMPLE_ID))

# cpm counts
cpmcounts_entrezid_relevancefilter_sumProbes_libSize = cpmcounts_entrezid_relevancefilter_sumProbes %>%
    select(gene_symbol_entrez_id, all_of(metadata_libSize$SAMPLE_ID))

# log2cpm counts
log2cpmcounts_entrezid_relevanceFilter_sumProbes_libSize = log2cpmcounts_entrezid_relevanceFilter_sumProbes %>%
    select(gene_symbol_entrez_id, all_of(metadata_libSize$SAMPLE_ID))

2.2.2 STEP 6: Replicate correlation (rCOR)

We perform replicate correlation on the log2cpm counts, to minimize the effect of outliers.

2.2.2.1 Pairwise

Calculate replicate correlation for each pair combination

replicateFilterOutput <- data.frame()

for (i in unique(metadata_libSize$TREATMENT_ID)) {
    metaDataReps <- metadata_libSize %>%
        filter(TREATMENT_ID == i)

    if (nrow(metaDataReps) > 1) {
        log2cpmDataReps <- log2cpmcounts_entrezid_relevanceFilter_sumProbes_libSize[,
            metaDataReps$SAMPLE_ID]

        colnames(log2cpmDataReps)

        for (j in 1:ncol(log2cpmDataReps)) {
            for (k in 1:ncol(log2cpmDataReps)) {
                sample_A <- colnames(log2cpmDataReps)[j]
                sample_B <- colnames(log2cpmDataReps)[k]

                if (sample_A != sample_B) {

                  paste0("Looking at ", sample_A, " vs. ", sample_B)

                  r2 <- cor(log2cpmDataReps[, j], log2cpmDataReps[, k])

                  replicateFilterOutput <- rbind(replicateFilterOutput, data.frame(TREATMENT_ID = i,
                    sample_A = sample_A, sample_B = sample_B, comp = paste0(sample_A,
                      " v. ", sample_B), r2 = r2))
                }
            }
        }
    }
}

replicateFilterOutput <- replicateFilterOutput %>%
    arrange(TREATMENT_ID)

head(replicateFilterOutput)

Calculate the highest replicate correlation for each sample

replicateFilterOutput <- replicateFilterOutput %>%
    separate(TREATMENT_ID, into = c("cell_line", "compound", "time", "concentration"),
        sep = "_", remove = FALSE) %>%
    group_by(sample_A) %>%
    mutate(max_r2 = max(r2, na.rm = T), factor_conc = as.numeric(gsub("uM", "", concentration)),
        concentration = factor(concentration, levels = c("0uM", "0.1uM", "0.5uM",
            "1uM", "2.5uM", "5uM", "10uM", "20uM", "30uM", "50uM")), time = factor(time,
            levels = c("0hr", "4hr", "8hr", "16hr", "24hr", "48hr", "72hr"))) %>%
    ungroup()

loop <- unique(replicateFilterOutput$time)
loop
## [1] 16hr 24hr 48hr 4hr  72hr 8hr  0hr 
## Levels: 0hr 4hr 8hr 16hr 24hr 48hr 72hr

Look at each timepoint

for (i in loop) {
    print(replicateFilterOutput %>%
        filter(time %in% i) %>%
        ggplot(aes(x = reorder(sample_A, factor_conc), y = r2)) + geom_boxplot(color = "grey90") +
        geom_point(color = "grey60") + geom_point(aes(y = max_r2, fill = concentration),
        color = "grey60", shape = 21, size = 3) + ylab("") + xlab("Sample ID") +
        ggtitle(i) + theme_bw() + theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(), legend.position = "none",
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 18)) + facet_wrap(~time +
        concentration, scales = "free_x", nrow = 1))
}

Look at the distribution of maximum pairwise correlations

replicateFilterMax <- replicateFilterOutput %>%
    select(time, max_r2) %>%
    distinct()

replicateFilterMax %>%
    group_by(time) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    ggplot(aes(y = time, x = max_r2)) + geom_boxplot() + ylab("")

Some timepoints have less variation in max r2 than others, but we want to capture acceptable variability.

Make an IQR for each compound and then remove max r2 that are more than 3 IQR from the best. Flag samples as an outlier if their highest obtained replicate correlation is further beneath the experimental medi than the best performing replicate correlation.

replicateFilterDetect <- replicateFilterOutput %>%
    select(TREATMENT_ID, r2, max_r2) %>%
    distinct() %>%
    mutate(testsystem_IQR = 1.5 * IQR(r2)) %>%
    group_by(TREATMENT_ID) %>%
    mutate(experimental_max = max(max_r2), experimental_threshold = experimental_max -
        testsystem_IQR) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    select(-r2, -max_r2) %>%
    distinct()

replicateFilterNew <- left_join(replicateFilterOutput, replicateFilterDetect, by = "TREATMENT_ID") %>%
    distinct()

replicateFilterNew <- replicateFilterNew %>%
    mutate(keep_replicate = ifelse(max_r2 < experimental_threshold, "No", "Yes"))

for (i in loop) {
    print(replicateFilterNew %>%
        filter(time == i) %>%
        ggplot(aes(x = reorder(sample_A, -factor_conc))) + geom_boxplot(aes(y = r2),
        color = "grey80") + geom_hline(aes(yintercept = experimental_max), color = "grey70") +
        geom_hline(aes(yintercept = experimental_threshold), color = "grey70", linetype = "dashed") +
        geom_point(aes(y = r2), color = "grey80") + geom_point(aes(y = max_r2, color = keep_replicate)) +
        scale_color_manual(values = c(Yes = "#6DACBC", No = "#CF7976")) + ggtitle(i) +
        xlab("Sample") + ylab("") + theme_bw() + theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)) + facet_wrap(~time +
        concentration, scales = "free_x", nrow = 1))
}

cat("The replicate filter removes", replicateFilterNew %>%
    filter(keep_replicate == "No") %>%
    pull(sample_A) %>%
    unique() %>%
    length(), "samples.")
## The replicate filter removes 11 samples.

2.2.2.2 Remove samples

From metadata

keepSamples = replicateFilterNew %>%
    filter(keep_replicate == "Yes") %>%
    pull(sample_A) %>%
    unique()

metadata_libSize_repFilter = metadata_libSize %>%
    filter(SAMPLE_ID %in% keepSamples)

cat("The following samples have been removed due to low replicate correlation:\n",
    paste(metadata_libSize %>%
        filter(!SAMPLE_ID %in% keepSamples) %>%
        pull(SAMPLE_ID), collapse = ", "))
## The following samples have been removed due to low replicate correlation:
##  S_4h_Cis5_Rep1, S_8h_Cis9_Rep1, S_8h_Cis1_Rep1, S_16h_Cis9_Rep1, S_16h_Cis6_Rep1, S_48h_Cis9_Rep1, S_48h_Cis8_Rep1, S_48h_Cis7_Rep1, S_72h_Cis4_Rep1, S_72h_Cis3_Rep3, S_4h_DMEM_Rep1

From counts

# raw counts
rawcounts_entrezid_relevanceFilter_sumProbes_libSize_repFilter = rawcounts_entrezid_relevanceFilter_sumProbes_libSize %>%
    select(gene_symbol_entrez_id, metadata_libSize_repFilter$SAMPLE_ID)

# cpm counts
cpmcounts_entrezid_relevancefilter_sumProbes_libSize_repFilter = cpmcounts_entrezid_relevancefilter_sumProbes_libSize %>%
    select(gene_symbol_entrez_id, metadata_libSize_repFilter$SAMPLE_ID)

# log2cpm counts
log2cpmcounts_entrezid_relevanceFilter_sumProbes_libSize_repFilter = log2cpmcounts_entrezid_relevanceFilter_sumProbes_libSize %>%
    select(gene_symbol_entrez_id, metadata_libSize_repFilter$SAMPLE_ID)

2.2.3 STEP 7: Viable Cells (FrVC)

Flag samples with < 70% viable cells. For this dataset, this information is not available.

2.2.4 STEP 8: Fraction uniquely mapped reads (FMR)

Reject samples if percentage mapped is < 70%. For this dataset, this information is not available.

2.2.5 STEP 9: Number of genes with >= 5 uniquely mapped reads (Ncov 5)

Flag samples if less than 5000 genes have >= 5 uniquely mapped reads.

# create long df
rawcounts_entrezid_relevanceFilter_sumProbes_libSize_repFilter_long = rawcounts_entrezid_relevanceFilter_sumProbes_libSize_repFilter %>%
    pivot_longer(-gene_symbol_entrez_id, names_to = "SAMPLE_ID", values_to = "counts")

Ncov5_summary = rawcounts_entrezid_relevanceFilter_sumProbes_libSize_repFilter_long %>%
    group_by(SAMPLE_ID) %>%
    summarize(genesAbove5 = sum(counts > 5), lowQuality = genesAbove5 < 5000, .groups = "drop")

head(Ncov5_summary)
table(Ncov5_summary$lowQuality)
## 
## FALSE 
##   192
Ncov5_flag = Ncov5_summary %>%
    filter(lowQuality == TRUE) %>%
    pull(SAMPLE_ID)

2.2.6 STEP 10: Number of probes capture top 80% of signal in sample (Nsig 80)

Flag samples if < 1000 genes capture the top 80% of the signal in a sample.

Nsig80_summary = rawcounts_entrezid_relevanceFilter_sumProbes_libSize_repFilter_long %>%
    group_by(SAMPLE_ID) %>%
    arrange(SAMPLE_ID, desc(counts)) %>%
    mutate(cum_sum = cumsum(counts), total = sum(counts), cum_frac = cum_sum/total,
        gene_rank = row_number()) %>%
    filter(cum_frac >= 0.8) %>%
    summarize(Nsig80 = min(gene_rank), lowComplexity = Nsig80 < 1000, .groups = "drop")


head(Nsig80_summary)
table(Nsig80_summary$lowComplexity)
## 
## FALSE  TRUE 
##   190     2
Nsig80_flag = Nsig80_summary %>%
    filter(lowComplexity == TRUE) %>%
    pull(SAMPLE_ID)

2.2.7 Add sample flags to metadata

metadata_libSize_repFilter %>%
    mutate(flagged_sampleQC = case_when(SAMPLE_ID %in% Ncov5_flag ~ "Ncov5_flag",
        SAMPLE_ID %in% Nsig80_flag ~ "Nsig80_flag", .default = "passed")) -> metadata_libSize_repFilter

2.2.8 Check filters in PCA

Check the removed samples in PCA, do we see additional outliers, are these removed if we increase the libsize filter/adjust the replicate correlation?

Before the low quality samples have been removed

pcaData_beforeSampFilter = log2cpmcounts_entrezid_relevanceFilter_sumProbes %>% column_to_rownames("gene_symbol_entrez_id") %>% t() %>% as.data.frame() %>% rownames_to_column("SAMPLE_ID") %>% left_join(metadata, by = "SAMPLE_ID") %>%
  mutate(removed_sampleQC = if_else(SAMPLE_ID %in% metadata_libSize_repFilter$SAMPLE_ID, true = "passed", false = "notPassed"),
         Ncov5_flag = if_else(SAMPLE_ID %in% Ncov5_flag, true = "flag", false = "passed"),
         Nsig80_flag = if_else(SAMPLE_ID %in% Nsig80_flag, true = "flag", false = "passed"))

pcaOutput_beforeSampFilter = prcomp(pcaData_beforeSampFilter %>% column_to_rownames("SAMPLE_ID") %>% select(log2cpmcounts_entrezid_relevanceFilter_sumProbes$gene_symbol_entrez_id))

autoplot(
  pcaOutput_beforeSampFilter,
  data = pcaData_beforeSampFilter,
  color = "CONCENTRATION",
  shape = "TIMEPOINT",
  size = 2
) + # change to 3
  labs(title = "PCA gene expression (log2CPM)") +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(15, 16, 17, 18, 7, 8, 13)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = color25)

Color removed samples

autoplot(
  pcaOutput_beforeSampFilter,
  data = pcaData_beforeSampFilter,
  color = "removed_sampleQC",
  shape = "TIMEPOINT",
  size = 2
) + # change to 3
  labs(title = "PCA gene expression (log2CPM)") +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(15, 16, 17, 18, 7, 8, 13)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("firebrick", "forestgreen"),
                     breaks = c("notPassed", "passed"))

Check flagged samples

autoplot(
  pcaOutput_beforeSampFilter,
  data = pcaData_beforeSampFilter,
  color = "Nsig80_flag",
  shape = "TIMEPOINT",
  size = 2
) + # change to 3
  labs(title = "PCA gene expression (log2CPM)") +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(15, 16, 17, 18, 7, 8, 13)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("firebrick", "forestgreen"),
                     breaks = c("flag", "passed"))

After the low quality samples have been removed

pcaData_afterSampleFilter = log2cpmcounts_entrezid_relevanceFilter_sumProbes_libSize_repFilter %>% column_to_rownames("gene_symbol_entrez_id") %>% t() %>% as.data.frame() %>% rownames_to_column("SAMPLE_ID") %>% left_join(metadata_libSize_repFilter, by = "SAMPLE_ID")

pcaOutput_afterSampleFilter = prcomp(pcaData_afterSampleFilter %>% column_to_rownames("SAMPLE_ID") %>% select(log2cpmcounts_entrezid_relevanceFilter_sumProbes_libSize_repFilter$gene_symbol_entrez_id))

autoplot(
  pcaOutput_afterSampleFilter,
  data = pcaData_afterSampleFilter,
  color = "CONCENTRATION",
  shape = "TIMEPOINT",
  size = 2
) + # change to 3
  labs(title = "PCA gene expression (log2CPM)") +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(15, 16, 17, 18, 7, 8, 13)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = color25)

Color flagged samples after filter

autoplot(
  pcaOutput_afterSampleFilter,
  data = pcaData_afterSampleFilter,
  color = "flagged_sampleQC",
  shape = "TIMEPOINT",
  size = 2
) + # change to 3
  labs(title = "PCA gene expression (log2CPM)") +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(15, 16, 17, 18, 7, 8, 13)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("black", "orange", "purple"),
                     breaks = c("passed", "Nsig80_flag", "Ncov5_flag"))

The flagged samples are nicely clustered with their replicate, so for now I decided to keep them in.

2.2.9 Save data

save(
  # metadata
  metadata_libSize_repFilter,
  
  # raw counts
  rawcounts_entrezid_relevanceFilter_sumProbes_libSize_repFilter,
  
  # cpm counts
  cpmcounts_entrezid_relevancefilter_sumProbes_libSize_repFilter,
  
  # log2cpm counts
  log2cpmcounts_entrezid_relevanceFilter_sumProbes_libSize_repFilter,
  
  file = file.path(outputDir, "updatedVersion", paste0(studyNR, "_dataAfterSampleQC.RData"))
  
)
